ai.title: AI settings
ai.intro: Connect providers for text and image generation. API keys stay on the server and are never sent to the browser.

text.title: Text generation
text.description: Pick the provider for questions, answers and whole quizzes.

image.title: Image generation
image.description: Local image generation via ComfyUI / Z-Image.

generate.title: Generate with AI
generate.notConfigured: No text provider configured. Set up the AI first.

help.text: “Question from topic” creates a complete question with answers. “Distractors” fills empty answer fields with plausible wrong options. You always see a preview first and decide with “Apply” or “Discard”.

privacy.local: Local model on your server. Your inputs never leave the server.
privacy.claude: Sends your topics and question text to Anthropic (Claude). See their privacy policy: https://www.anthropic.com/legal/privacy
privacy.openai: Sends your topics and question text to OpenAI. See their privacy policy: https://openai.com/policies/privacy-policy
privacy.openrouter: Sends your topics and question text to OpenRouter and the selected model. See their privacy policy: https://openrouter.ai/privacy
privacy.external: Sends your topics and question text to an external service. Check its privacy policy.

temperature.label: Temperature
temperature.help: Higher = more creative, lower = more precise

resolution.label: Image resolution
resolution.help: Edge length of the generated image

satellite.title: Satellite Display
satellite.description: Attach a Raspberry Pi or a beamer PC as a big screen. The satellite shows the host view (lobby, questions, results) on the big screen, while you drive the whole game from your phone. No laptop needed at the party.
satellite.howto: Open razzoozle.joelduss.xyz/display on the beamer and enter the code it shows here.
satellite.step1: Connect the Pi or beamer to the internet and open razzoozle.joelduss.xyz/display — the big screen shows a pairing code.
satellite.step3: Your phone stays the remote: start, next and auto-mode are all driven from here.
satellite.hint: Pairing only works once a game is running — the code needs the live game and your manager password.
satellite.step1Result: The big screen then shows a 6-digit code like «ABC123».
satellite.step2Enter: Start a game and tap «Satellite» at the top of the game header. Enter the code shown on the big screen there.
satellite.step2Result: Tap «Pair» to test the connection. If it works, «Display paired» appears.
satellite.troubleCode: «Invalid or expired code»: The code is wrong or too old (it is valid for only 5 minutes). Reload the /display page on the big screen — it then shows a fresh code — and type it exactly as shown.
satellite.troubleNotConnected: «Display not connected»: The projector or Pi lost the connection. Check that it is still online and reload the /display page. On your own network a firewall may block it — then the ports for razzoozle.joelduss.xyz must be opened.
satellite.troubleGame: «Game not found» or «Invalid password»: Pairing only works while your game is running and you are signed in as the host. Restart the game or sign in again, then try once more right away.

sim.windowHint: Add bots in the lobby or between questions

theme.contrastNote: Light primary colors are automatically darkened for text and icons so they stay legible.

sounds.description: Upload a custom sound per slot. Empty = default sound.

theme.title: Design
theme.intro: Tailor colors, logo and branding to your school.

statusEmpty.headline: No suggestions in this view
statusEmpty.hint: Choose a different status to see more suggestions.

rejectReason.label: Reason (optional)
rejectReason.placeholder: Why is this question being rejected?

submissions.submitLinkUnavailable: Not yet available — your personal link will be activated soon.
submissions.intro: Review submitted questions and approve them.

selectQuizz.intro: Pick a quiz and start a game.

gameMode.title: Game mode
gameMode.description: In team mode, points are summed up per team and a team leaderboard is shown.
gameMode.lowLatencyDescription: Tunes timing for fast play: clock sync, answer acknowledgement and throttled leaderboard updates. Also shows the latency health panel for the host.
gameMode.klassenDescription: Enables class mode, where students join from a teacher-managed student directory.
gameMode.endScreenDescription: Select which end screen display options should be available to the teacher.
gameMode.lobbyDescription: When enabled, new players can no longer join the lobby. Existing players and their reconnections are unaffected.
gameMode.randomizeAnswersDescription: Randomly shuffles the order of answer options per question while keeping canonical indices for scoring.
gameMode.scoringDescription: Choose how points are calculated. Speed takes answer time into account, accuracy counts only correct or incorrect answers.

endScreenModeDescription.full: Shows the full leaderboard at the end of the game.
endScreenModeDescription.top3: Shows only the top three at the end of the game.
endScreenModeDescription.private: Results are visible to the teacher only.

gameMode.endScreenMinOneActive: At least one end screen option must remain active.

achievementsConfig.hint: Enable or disable badges, customize their names and descriptions, and adjust numeric thresholds.

thresholdHint.minPointsAhead: Point lead of the overtaken player

exit.description: All players will be notified and the game will end.

aiKeys.description: Set up your own API key for AI generation. Without your own key, the instance default will be used if available. A stored key will never be shown again.
aiKeys.empty: The administrator has not set up any external AI providers yet.
